headline: Gerard Piqué Is Rebuilding Football From the Ground Up
dek: Kings League has gone from a viral experiment to a €200M media and sports property — and Piqué is just getting started.
author: Sofía Martínez
body: Gerard Piqué won eight La Liga titles, three Champions Leagues, and a World Cup. Then he retired from football at 35 and built something that might matter more. Kings League — the 7-a-side format he co-founded in 2022 with streamer Ibai Llanos — has become one of the fastest-growing sports properties in Europe, blending live sport, streaming culture, and creator audiences in a format that legacy institutions cannot replicate.

The league closed a €40M sponsorship cycle in Q1 2026, with anchor partners including Puma, Spotify, and Neon. Broadcast rights have been sold in 14 territories. The Kings World Cup in March 2026 drew 92,000 live attendees in Barcelona's Estadi Olímpic and 8.3M concurrent viewers on Twitch — the platform's largest single sports event since Ninja's retirement stream. Kosmos Holding, Piqué's investment vehicle, now manages €200M+ across football IP, Davis Cup media rights, and four European tech bets.

Piqué is expanding Kings League into Italy, Germany, and Mexico in H2 2026 — each market structured as a joint venture with a local media partner. A women's league, Queens League, is already in its second season. The pitch to investors: sport built natively for streaming, not retrofitted for it.
quote: Traditional football told fans to sit down and watch. We told them to become part of the product. That's not a gimmick — that's the only model that scales for the next generation.
quote_author: Gerard Piqué, Founder, Kings League / Kosmos Holding

headline: Mbappé Launches €30M European Sports Tech Fund
body: Kylian Mbappé has formally launched MM Ventures, a Paris-based early-stage fund targeting European sports technology, health, and creator economy startups. The fund has already made five seed investments, including French performance analytics platform AthleteIQ and a Berlin-based fan engagement SaaS.

headline: McGregor's Proper No. Twelve Gets €50M Distillery Expansion
body: Conor McGregor's Proper No. Twelve Irish whiskey brand — which he sold a majority stake in to Becle for $600M in 2021 — is investing €50M in a new County Kildare distillery set to open in 2027. McGregor retains a minority equity stake and remains active on brand strategy.

headline: Your Name Gets You the First Meeting. Your Operator Gets You the Second.
body: Too many athlete-founded companies are stuck at Series A because the founder is the entire commercial team. The ones that scale — SpringHill, Proper No. Twelve, Kings League — all hired a professional operator within 18 months of launch. Your equity is the asset. Hire someone to protect it.

headline: Richie McCaw Backs NZ Sports Analytics Platform Kontrol
body: New Zealand Rugby legend Richie McCaw has led a NZ$4M seed round into Kontrol, an Auckland-based platform that provides real-time biomechanical analysis for elite rugby academies. McCaw cited injury prevention as the core thesis — Kontrol's AI flags movement patterns linked to ACL risk before symptoms emerge.

headline: Antoine Griezmann's Esports Org Holyfrag Hits 1M YouTube Subscribers
body: Antoine Griezmann's esports and content organisation Holyfrag crossed one million YouTube subscribers this week, less than 18 months after launch. The org now fields competitive rosters in Valorant, EA FC, and Rocket League. Griezmann, a lifelong gamer, holds a 40% equity stake and is actively involved in content direction.

headline: Fernando Alonso Takes Equity Stake in Spanish Mobility Startup Kairos
body: Two-time Formula 1 World Champion Fernando Alonso has taken an undisclosed equity stake in Kairos, a Madrid-based EV fleet management platform targeting logistics operators across Southern Europe. Alonso joins as a strategic advisor, leveraging his motorsport engineering network. Kairos has raised €12M to date.

headline: Tadej Pogačar Launches TDF Nutrition — Performance Fueling for Amateurs
body: Tour de France winner Tadej Pogačar has co-founded TDF Nutrition, a sports fueling brand built on the carbohydrate and electrolyte protocols used by Team UAE during his 2025 and 2024 Grand Tour campaigns. The brand launches direct-to-consumer across Europe in May 2026, with a €5M seed round backed by Unilever Ventures.

headline: Novak Djokovic's ND Nutrition Expands to UK and Germany
body: Novak Djokovic's plant-based nutrition brand ND Nutrition — built on the gluten-free, dairy-free protocols that drove his late-career dominance — is expanding into the UK and German markets following a successful rollout across the Balkans and Austria. The brand secured shelf space in Holland & Barrett and dm Drogerie Markt ahead of a summer launch.
